metadata.description: Top-5 most recent sessions at the moment this iteration completed. Reviewers use this to verify KB state and adaptive prompting grounds in real data.

questions.question_text: What is the name of the section?
questions.explanation: B is correct because the text explicitly states 'SECTION: Operational Tactics and Combat Doctrine'.

questions.question_text: What type of knowledge is the section intended to measure?
questions.explanation: B is correct because the requirements specify that each question should 'test genuine understanding, not surface memorization'.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is NOT a requirement for the questions?

choices.A: Each question must be clear and specific
choices.C: The correct answer must be obvious

questions.explanation: C is correct because the requirements explicitly state that 'All 4 choices must be plausible (no obviously wrong options)', which is the opposite of option C.

questions.question_text: What is the purpose of varying the difficulty of the questions?

choices.A: To make the assessment more challenging
choices.B: To provide a range of question types
choices.C: To cover different topics and concepts
choices.D: To increase the time required to complete the assessment

questions.explanation: C is correct because the requirements state that 'no two questions should test the same idea' and 'questions should vary in difficulty'.

questions.question_text: What does the section heading imply about the content of the section?

choices.A: It will cover operational plans and tactics
choices.B: It will focus on combat doctrine and operational tactics
choices.C: It will cover only combat operations
choices.D: It will focus on tactical doctrine

questions.explanation: B is correct because the section heading explicitly states 'Operational Tactics and Combat Doctrine', implying a focus on both topics.

questions.question_text: What types of information are documented in case files?
questions.explanation: B is correct because the text specifically mentions 'Case Files: Documented Engagements and Incidents'.

choices.C: Remote areas outside of operational territory
choices.D: Public areas with high foot traffic

questions.explanation: C is correct because the text implies safehouses are located outside of known operational territory, as they are referred to as 'safehouses'.

questions.question_text: What do the listed sections suggest about the organization's focus?

choices.A: Logistical Support and Supply Chain Management

questions.explanation: B is correct because the listed sections, such as 'Known Bases, Safehouses, and Operational Territory', suggest a focus on planning and executing tactical operations.

questions.question_text: What is the purpose of listing known bases and safehouses?

choices.A: To conceal the organization's operational territory
choices.B: To document and secure the organization's operational territory
choices.C: To plan and coordinate tactical operations
choices.D: To manage the organization's logistical supply chain

questions.explanation: B is correct because listing known bases and safehouses would allow the organization to document and secure their operational territory.

questions.question_text: What is implied about the organization's operational territory?

choices.A: It is highly secure and well-defended
choices.B: It is highly classified and restricted
choices.D: It is constantly changing and dynamic

questions.explanation: C is correct because the text lists 'Known Bases, Safehouses, and Operational Territory', implying that the operational territory is known and documented.
